session.tool_result:
50→- **[gateway-inventory.md](gateway-inventory.md)** — understanding available gateways, adding a new gateway 51→- **[gateway-removal-pattern.md](gateway-removal-pattern.md)** — Consolidating two gateways into one, Removing deprecated gateway implementations, Refactoring gateway hierarchies 52→- **[gateway-signature-migration.md](gateway-signature-migration.md)** — changing gateway method signatures, migrating callers after gateway API changes, updating discriminated union return types across call sites 53→- **[gateway-specific-patterns.md](gateway-specific-patterns.md)** — considering mixing discriminated unions with exceptions in gateway methods, designing operations with both expected failures and cleanup steps, reviewing historical gateway error handling experiments 54→- **[gateway-vs-backend.md](gateway-vs-backend.md)** — creating a new ABC interface, deciding between gateway and backend patterns, understanding PlanBackend vs Git gateway architecture, choosing the right abstraction pattern for a new service 55→- **[generated-files.md](generated-files.md)** — understanding how agent docs sync works, debugging generated file issues, adding new generated file types 56→- **[git-graphite-quirks.md](git-graphite-quirks.md)** — debugging unexpected git/gt behavior, handling rebase/restack edge cases, writing conflict detection logic, troubleshooting detached HEAD states, handling concurrent worktree operations, understanding worktree lock files 57→- **[git-operation-patterns.md](git-operation-patterns.md)** — implementing git operations in gateways, checking if git branches or refs exist, deciding between LBYL and EAFP for git commands 58→- **[git-plumbing-patterns.md](git-plumbing-patterns.md)** — modifying plan_save.py branch commit behavior, understanding git plumbing patterns in erk, working with commit_files_to_branch, eliminating git checkouts, dispatch without checkout 59→- **[github-admin-gateway.md](github-admin-gateway.md)** — working with GitHub repository secrets or admin operations, adding methods to the GitHubAdmin gateway, understanding the 4-place gateway pattern with security-sensitive operations 60→- **[github-api-diagnostics.md](github-api-diagnostics.md)** — debugging GitHub API failures, investigating repository-specific API issues, GitHub CLI commands returning unexpected errors 61→- **[github-api-rate-limits.md](github-api-rate-limits.md)** — using gh CLI commands programmatically, encountering GraphQL rate limit errors, choosing between REST and GraphQL API, implementing GitHub API calls in gateways 62→- **[github-api-retry-mechanism.md](github-api-retry-mechanism.md)** — implementing GitHub API calls with retry logic, handling transient network errors, using execute_gh_command_with_retry(), injecting Time for testable retry delays 63→- **[github-cli-limits.md](github-cli-limits.md)** — using gh pr diff in production code, working with large pull requests (300+ files), encountering HTTP 406 errors from gh CLI, implementing PR file discovery, working with GitHub codespaces in code, implementing codespace operations 64→- **[github-cli-quirks.md](github-cli-quirks.md)** — using gh gist create with --filename flag, debugging unexpected gh CLI behavior, working with gh gist commands 65→- **[github-gist-api.md](github-gist-api.md)** — constructing gist raw URLs, downloading gist content programmatically, working with single-file gists 66→- **[github-graphql-label-semantics.md](github-graphql-label-semantics.md)** — querying GitHub issues or PRs with multiple label filters, debugging unexpected empty results from GitHub GraphQL label queries, implementing label-based filtering for plan or issue views 67→- **[github-graphql.md](github-graphql.md)** — using gh api graphql, writing GraphQL queries for GitHub, passing variables to GraphQL queries, fetching data not available in REST API 68→- **[github-interface-patterns.md](github-interface-patterns.md)** — calling GitHub API from erk, working with gh api command, fetching PR or issue data efficiently, understanding PRDetails type 69→- **[github-issue-autoclose.md](github-issue-autoclose.md)** — understanding why erk no longer uses Closes #N, understanding plan closure strategy 70→- **[github-parsing.md](github-parsing.md)** — parsing GitHub URLs, extracting PR or issue numbers from URLs, understanding github parsing layers 71→- **[globalconfig-field-addition.md](globalconfig-field-addition.md)** — adding a new field to GlobalConfig, extending erk's global configuration, adding a user-configurable setting to ~/.erk/config.toml 72→- **[graphite-cache-invalidation.md](graphite-cache-invalidation.md)** — implementing mtime-based cache invalidation, caching Graphite branch metadata, optimizing repeated calls to git or graphite operations 73→- **[health-check-runner-gateway.md](health-check-runner-gateway.md)** — working with health check infrastructure, modifying doctor command, adding new health checks, working with artifact allowlist 74→- **[hook-marker-detection.md](hook-marker-detection.md)** — Adding a new hook managed by erk, Implementing version detection for artifacts, Understanding how hook updates work 75→- **[http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md](http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md)** — understanding HTTP-only architecture for plan data fetching, working with HttpClient ABC extensions, optimizing plan list performance, adding CLI commands that need http_client 76→- **[impl-context-api.md](impl-context-api.md)** — working with .erk/impl-context/ folder, understanding plan submission staging, creating or removing impl-context directories 77→- **[impl-folder-discovery.md](impl-folder-discovery.md)** — working with .erk/impl-context/ directory resolution, debugging why a plan wasn't found during implementation setup, writing tests that use branch-scoped impl-context paths, understanding the difference between impl_folder.py and impl_context.py 78→- **[impl-folder-lifecycle.md](impl-folder-lifecycle.md)** — working with .erk/impl-context/ folders, understanding remote implementation workflow, debugging plan visibility in PRs 79→- **[inference-hoisting.md](inference-hoisting.md)** — adding LLM calls to an exec script, calling PromptExecutor from a CLI command or exec script, working with BranchSlugGenerator or generate_branch_slug, adding --branch-slug or similar pre-computed value flags to exec commands, understanding why exec scripts must be deterministic, refactoring nested LLM calls out of exec scripts, working with plan summary generation or --summary flags

91→- **[multi-worktree-state.md](multi-worktree-state.md)** — checkout operations in multi-worktree repositories, landing PRs with worktree cleanup, understanding git single-checkout constraint, debugging checkout failures across worktrees 93→- **[not-found-sentinel.md](not-found-sentinel.md)** — designing return types for lookup operations, handling missing resource cases without exceptions, checking if get_pr_for_branch() returned a PR, working with GitHub PR lookup results 96→- **[parameter-threading-pattern.md](parameter-threading-pattern.md)** — adding parameters to multi-layer commands (skill → command → exec), working with slash commands that call erk exec, debugging "No such option" errors in commands

1→--- 2→title: Rebase Confirmation Workflow 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying the erk pr rebase command" 5→ - "adding conflict confirmation UI to CLI commands" 6→ - "understanding rebase convergence paths" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "launching Claude for conflict resolution without showing conflicted files first" 9→ warning: "Always show conflicted files and get user confirmation before launching Claude. See rebase-confirmation-workflow.md." 10→--- 11→ 12→# Rebase Confirmation Workflow 13→ 14→The `erk pr rebase` command shows conflicted files and gets user confirmation before launching Claude for conflict resolution. This prevents users from being dropped into a Claude session without knowing what needs resolving. 15→ 16→## Three Convergence Paths 17→ 18→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rebase_cmd.py --> 19→ 20→All three entry paths converge at the same confirmation point: 21→ 22→### 1. Graphite Restack Path 23→ 24→When Graphite is enabled, uses `gt restack --no-interactive`. If restack succeeds (no conflicts), returns immediately. If conflicts remain, falls through to confirmation. 25→ 26→### 2. Git Rebase Path 27→ 28→When Graphite is not enabled and no rebase is in progress, requires `--target` flag. Runs `git rebase` onto target. If rebase succeeds, returns. If conflicts remain, falls through to confirmation. 29→ 30→### 3. In-Progress Rebase Path
... [34 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Resolve conflicts from an in-progress rebase using Claude TUI.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 6→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 7→ 8→ 9→@click.command("resolve-conflicts") 10→@click.option( 11→ "-d", 12→ "--dangerous", 13→ is_flag=True, 14→ help="Force dangerous mode (skip permission prompts).", 15→) 16→@click.option( 17→ "--safe", 18→ is_flag=True, 19→ help="Disable dangerous mode (permission prompts enabled).", 20→) 21→@click.pass_obj 22→def resolve_conflicts(ctx: ErkContext, *, dangerous: bool, safe: bool) -> None: 23→ """Resolve merge conflicts from an in-progress rebase using Claude TUI. 24→ 25→ This command resolves conflicts from a rebase already in progress. It does NOT 26→ initiate a rebase. Start the rebase yourself with 'git rebase <branch>', 27→ 'gt restack', etc., then run this command when conflicts arise. 28→ 29→ Examples: 30→
... [64 lines omitted] ...

It requires an active rebase already in progress. Users start the rebase themselves (git rebase, gt restack), then call this command." 12→--- 13→ 14→# Resolve Conflicts Workflow 15→ 16→The `erk pr resolve-conflicts` command resolves merge conflicts from an **already in-progress rebase**. It does not initiate a rebase. 17→ 18→## Source 19→ 20→`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/resolve_conflicts_cmd.py` 21→ 22→## Scope 23→ 24→This command requires a rebase already in progress. If no rebase is detected, it exits with an error: 25→ 26→``` 27→No rebase in progress. Start a rebase first with 'git rebase <branch>', 28→'gt restack', etc., then run this command when conflicts arise. 29→```

1→--- 2→audit_result: clean 3→last_audited: "2026-02-17 16:00 PT" 4→read_when: 5→ - triggering GitHub Actions workflows from CLI 6→ - adding a new workflow to erk launch 7→ - understanding local vs remote command duality 8→title: Workflow Commands 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP maps command names to .yml filenames 11→ warning: command names intentionally diverge from filenames (e.g., pr-rebase 12→ → pr-rebase.yml, but plan-implement → plan-implement.yml via DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME 13→ constant) 14→ - action: 15→ plan-implement exists in WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP but erk launch plan-implement 16→ always raises UsageError 17→ warning: use erk pr submit instead 18→ - action: using this pattern 19→ warning: PR workflows automatically update plan dispatch metadata when the 20→ branch follows the plnd/ prefix naming pattern 21→--- 22→ 23→# Workflow Commands 24→ 25→## Design Decisions 26→ 27→### Why a Unified Launcher 28→ 29→`erk launch` consolidates all GitHub Actions workflow triggers behind a single command instead of scattering them across subcommand groups (e.g., `erk pr rebase-remote`, `erk workflow launch`). This was a deliberate migration away from the old pattern where each remote operation had its own command in the relevant noun group. 30→
... [56 lines omitted] ...
44→`plan-implement` exists in `WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP` but `erk launch plan-implement` always raises `UsageError` directing users to `erk pr submit`. The plan-implement workflow requires branch creation, PR setup, and metadata initialization that `erk pr submit` handles as a coordinated sequence. Triggering the workflow directly would skip these prerequisites and produce orphaned workflow runs.

74→| "Claude CLI is required" | Install Claude from claude.com/download | 75→ 76→## Relationship to Other Commands 77→ 78→- `erk pr rebase` - Fix conflicts in merge state (not divergence) 79→- `gt submit` - What you retry after diverge-fix succeeds 80→- `/erk:diverge-fix` - The slash command this wraps 81→

50→ ``` 51→3. The regenerated file will be correct based on current frontmatter state 52→ 53→## Two-Phase Rebase Pattern 54→ 55→`erk pr rebase` uses a two-phase approach: 56→ 57→**Phase 1: Mechanical rebase** — Attempts an automated rebase without human intervention: 58→ 59→- If Graphite is enabled and the branch is tracked: runs `gt restack --no-interactive` 60→- If Graphite is disabled: runs `git rebase <target>` (requires `--target` option) 61→ 62→**Phase 2: Claude TUI fallback** — If Phase 1 hits conflicts, launches Claude Code interactively with `/erk:pr-rebase` for AI-assisted conflict resolution via `execute_interactive()`. 63→ 64→The Phase 1 strategy selection checks whether Graphite is enabled by inspecting the graphite context object. See the Phase 1 dispatch logic in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rebase_cmd.py`.
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. session.assistant: Now for the rebase-conflict-patterns.md, the "Two-Phase Rebase Pattern" documents the old behavior. The command is now replaced:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Rebase Conflict Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "resolving merge conflicts after rebase" 5→ - "debugging test failures after rebase" 6→ - "handling auto-generated file conflicts" 7→ - "resuming a rebase with conflicts" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "running tests immediately after rebase without checking for old symbols" 10→ warning: "Hidden regressions can exist in non-conflicted files. Grep for old symbols that should have been renamed before running tests." 11→--- 12→ 13→# Rebase Conflict Patterns 14→ 15→Rebase operations can introduce subtle regressions in files that don't show as conflicts. This document covers patterns for preventing and detecting these issues. 16→ 17→## Hidden Regressions in Non-Conflicted Files 18→ 19→After a rebase, git marks files with textual conflicts for manual resolution. However, **semantic** conflicts in non-conflicted files go undetected: 20→ 21→- A function was renamed in the base branch, but call sites in your branch still use the old name 22→- A type signature changed, but your branch's callers pass the old argument types 23→- A module was moved, but your branch imports from the old location 24→ 25→These don't produce merge markers — git considers the files cleanly merged because the changes don't overlap textually. 26→ 27→## Prevention: Grep Before Running Tests 28→ 29→After resolving all textual conflicts: 30→
... [49 lines omitted] ...

session.tool_result: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

1→--- 2→title: Merge Conflict Resolution for Import Consolidation 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:30 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "resolving merge conflicts during rebase" 7→ - "fixing import conflicts after consolidation" 8→ - "rebasing after shared module changes" 9→--- 10→ 11→# Merge Conflict Resolution for Import Consolidation 12→ 13→When import paths are consolidated into shared modules, rebasing other branches can cause merge conflicts. This guide covers the resolution strategy. 14→ 15→## Common Scenario 16→ 17→You consolidated imports in one branch: 18→ 19→```python 20→# Before: multiple local helpers 21→from .local_helpers import parse_url 22→ 23→# After: shared module 24→from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import parse_url 25→``` 26→ 27→When rebasing other branches that still use the old imports, conflicts appear. 28→ 29→## Resolution Strategy 30→
... [105 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Workflow Run List 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying workflow run list display" 5→ - "working with erk workflow run list" 6→ - "understanding run-name format parsing" 7→ - "modifying workflow run display" 8→--- 9→ 10→# Workflow Run List 11→ 12→The `erk workflow run list` command displays GitHub Actions workflow runs in a PR-centric table view. 13→ 14→## Command Hierarchy 15→ 16→Moved from top-level `erk run` to `erk workflow run list` and `erk workflow run logs` in PR #8549. 17→ 18→## Source Files 19→ 20→- `src/erk/cli/commands/run/list_cmd.py` -- Main list command (192 lines) 21→- `src/erk/cli/commands/run/shared.py` -- Shared utilities (42 lines) 22→- `src/erk/cli/commands/doctor_workflow.py:253` -- Registration point 23→ 24→## PR-Centric Display 25→ 26→Runs are displayed with PR numbers extracted from run-name format. The table columns are: run-id, status, submitted, workflow, pr, title, chks. 27→ 28→## Run-Name Format Parsing 29→ 30→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/run/shared.py:6-23 -->
... [50 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: PlanRowData Field Reference 3→read_when: 4→ - "writing command availability predicates" 5→ - "understanding what data is available for TUI commands" 6→ - "checking which PlanRowData fields are nullable" 7→last_audited: "2026-02-23 00:15 PT" 8→audit_result: edited 9→--- 10→ 11→# PlanRowData Field Reference 12→ 13→Quick reference for writing command availability predicates and understanding 14→`PlanRowData` usage patterns. 15→ 16→## Overview 17→ 18→`PlanRowData` is a frozen dataclass containing all data for a single plan row in the TUI. 1→"""Data types for TUI components.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→import dataclasses 6→from dataclasses import dataclass 7→from datetime import datetime 8→from typing import Any 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import IssueFilterState 11→ 12→ 13→@dataclass(frozen=True) 14→class PrRowData: 15→ """Row data for displaying a plan in the TUI table. 16→ 17→ Contains pre-formatted display strings and raw data needed for actions. 18→ Immutable to ensure table state consistency. 19→ 20→ **Display vs Raw convention:** Many data points have both a raw field (for 21→ logic/predicates) and a display field (for table rendering). Raw fields are 22→ often nullable (None when absent), while display fields are always strings 23→ (dash "-" or empty when absent). Use raw fields in availability predicates 24→ (``ctx.row.pr_number is not None``), display fields for rendering. 25→ 26→ Attributes: 27→ 28→ pr_number: GitHub issue/PR number (e.g., 123). Never None. 29→ pr_url: URL to PR (GitHub or Graphite), None if unavailable. 30→ full_title: Complete untruncated title. Empty string possible.
... [105 lines omitted] ...

`parse_plan_number` doesn't exist in the current codebase. The `import-conflict-resolution.md` doc has examples showing it as a function that used to exist. The doc's conflict example shows `parse_plan_number` being imported from `erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing` - this is an example of a conflict scenario, not a description of a currently-existing function. The plan says to update if it was renamed to `parse_pr_number`.
Looking at the doc more carefully: it's showing a sample conflict - the function name `parse_plan_number` appears in the _example conflict scenario_ (lines 37 and 58). The doc is teaching a conflict resolution strategy using that as an example. The actual resolution shown in the doc uses `parse_plan_number` in the final resolution - if this no longer exists, the example should be updated.
